title: Prototype Decision Algorithm
description: Algorithm to help decide between horizontal (breadth-first) and vertical (depth-first) prototyping approaches based on project phase and objectives
code:
PROTOTYPE_DECISION(projectPhase, objective, resources)
BEGIN
 IF projectPhase == "discovery" OR projectPhase == "concept" THEN
 RETURN "horizontal prototype"
 // Goal: validate overall concept, get stakeholder buy-in
 ELSE IF projectPhase == "development" AND objective == "testComplexFlow" THEN
 RETURN "vertical prototype"
 // Goal: test specific feature deeply
 ELSE IF projectPhase == "testing" THEN
 RETURN "high-fidelity interactive prototype"
 // Goal: detailed usability testing
 ELSE IF resources == "limited" THEN
 RETURN "paper prototype"
 // Quick and inexpensive
 ELSE IF objective == "informationArchitecture" THEN
 RETURN "horizontal prototype"
 ELSE
 RETURN "balanced prototype"
 END IF
END
explanation: This algorithm helps designers choose the appropriate prototype type based on where they are in the project lifecycle and what they hope to achieve. Early phases favor breadth, later phases favor depth.

title: Fidelity Level Selection
description: Decision logic for choosing the appropriate fidelity level for a prototype based on feedback goals and timeline
code:
SELECT_FIDELITY(feedbackNeeded, timeline, stakeholders)
BEGIN
 score = 0
 
 // Score based on feedback type
 IF feedbackNeeded == "concept" THEN score = score + 1
 ELSE IF feedbackNeeded == "usability" THEN score = score + 3
 ELSE IF feedbackNeeded == "visual" THEN score = score + 4
 
 // Score based on timeline
 IF timeline == "urgent" THEN score = score - 2
 ELSE IF timeline == "flexible" THEN score = score + 1
 
 // Score based on stakeholders
 IF stakeholders == "internal" THEN score = score - 1
 ELSE IF stakeholders == "external" THEN score = score + 2
 
 // Return fidelity based on total score
 IF score <= 2 THEN RETURN "low-fidelity"
 ELSE IF score <= 5 THEN RETURN "medium-fidelity"
 ELSE RETURN "high-fidelity"
END
explanation: This algorithm calculates a score based on multiple factors to recommend the appropriate fidelity level. Higher scores indicate need for more polished, realistic prototypes.
